existingTestSetup.coverageTool: go test -coverprofile (wired in CI, not run locally by default)

transcript.text: what does this project do / who uses it?

It's an MCP server, written in Go, that exposes a couple of filesystem/shell tools over stdio. Whatever LLM agent host connects to it — Claude Code, Cursor, some other MCP client — calls our tools. Used by developers wiring agent tooling into their editor or CLI.
does this server itself make runtime LLM calls or run agentic behavior, or is it purely a tool provider invoked by something else's agent?

Purely a tool provider. We never call an LLM API from this process — the model lives entirely in whatever client connects to us over MCP; we just execute the tool call it sends and hand back a result.
was this codebase itself authored by an AI coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor, etc. writing the code), separate from what the server does at runtime?
No, this one's hand-written by our team. The AGENTS.md file is there so contributors who do use a coding agent have a stopping condition, but the code wasn't generated by one.
Runs as a subprocess over stdio, spawned by the MCP client. No network listener, no browser context.
None. It's an internal developer tool, not customer-facing.
what coverage percentage should gate-6 enforce?
80% is fine, same as our other Go services.

exceedsBaseline.note: Zero runtime dependencies — no third-party MCP SDK or any other module required; JSON-RPC framing and tool dispatch are built directly on the Go standard library

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
out=$(govulncheck ./... 2>/dev/null || true)
while IFS= read -r vuln; do
  [ -z "$vuln" ] && continue
  echo -e "go.mod\t${vuln}"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'Vulnerability #[0-9]+: .*' || true)
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
out=$(go vet ./... 2>&1 || true)
while IFS=: read -r file line rest; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  echo -e "${file}:${line}\t${rest# }"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -E '^\./.*\.go:[0-9]+:' || true)
while IFS=: read -r file line _; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  echo -e "${file}:${line}\tstub implementation left in exported function"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(grep -rn --include='*.go' -B1 'panic("not implemented")' . | grep -oE '^[^ ]+\.go-[0-9]+' | tr '-' ':' || true)
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  echo -e "${file}:${line}\t${msg}"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(grep -rn --include='*.go' -B2 'exec.Command("sh", "-c"' . | grep -oE '^[^ ]+\.go-[0-9]+' | tr '-' ':' || true)
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
out=$(go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... 2>&1 || true)
while IFS= read -r line; do
  echo -e "cmd/server\tgo test failure: ${line}"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -E '^--- FAIL' || true)
pct=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | awk '{print $NF}' | tr -d '%')
if [ -n "$pct" ] && awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct < 80)}"; then
  echo -e "coverage.out\tcoverage ${pct}% below 80% threshold"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
fi
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
if [ ! -f AGENTS.md ]; then
  echo -e "AGENTS.md\tmissing agent context file at repo root"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
else
  grep -q "karen-mcp-go" AGENTS.md || {
    echo -e "AGENTS.md\tmissing project-identity summary agents need before making changes"
    issues=$((issues + 1))
  }
fi
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
